title: 烟囱别墅之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\142-烟囱别墅之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Virginia and Anthony discuss whether the Red Hand Gang might be responsible for the murder of Michael. After rowing back to shore, they reunite with the Earl of Carrington and Lady Bander. During their conversation, Lady Bander reveals that a French governess named Miss White has been residing in the second room from the left for two months, a detail that Anthony finds suspicious.

As a taxi approaches the villa, Anthony recognizes a passenger as his old friend, Baron Lolopraitcher, also known as Lolly. Following the departure of Bunter, Anthony hears a suspicious sound near the boathouse and discovers a tall, bespectacled man with a pointed black beard attempting to hide. The stranger claims to be lost and says he is seeking directions to the Happy Wanderer Inn, though he mentions he is staying at the Happy Cricketer's Inn. Anthony finds the man's presence highly suspicious, noting the coincidence of another mysterious Frenchman appearing near the room occupied by the French governess, and wonders if the man is involved in the Hessian uprising.

Upon returning to the villa, the Duke of Carrington introduces Anthony to the Baron and Captain Andreas. Anthony pulls the Baron aside to confess that he had previously used the false name James McGlashan and reveals that he is the author of the memoirs the Baron had been seeking. The Baron explains that the Prince had orchestrated the retrieval of the manuscripts with the intention of reading and then burning them. The Baron suggests that the memoirs have likely already been burned, though he admits this is only a guess.
